---
title: "GPT for social scientists?"
description: "Don't waste 3 years to develop a scale or collect data."
author: "Yanto Chandra"
date: "17 April 2026"
image: "cover.jpg"
categories:
  - Science
  - Data
---

In early 2023, I and my collaborator have worked really hard to design a methodological framework using GPT for scale development and synthetic data generation to measure, predict, retrodict, and calibrate innovation outcomes using real-world and synthetic data and a human-in-the-loop process in competitive settings, where many compete but few will be winners.

After multiple rounds with super critical reviewers, this work was published finally (still early view) in ***Public Administration Review***, an excellent journal in public administration. <a href="https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/puar.70098" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.7rem; border-radius: 2rem; gap: 0.2rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: center;"> <i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Article </a>
